steps_en: Mix the essential oils with the 90° alcohol.
Allow the filtered liqueur to rest for a long time.

yield.note: yield varies according to the alcohol strength of the wine used

steps_en: Crush 2 parts of apricot kernels and remove their shells.
Place the kernels in maceration in 100 parts of generous (fortified) wine.
The next day, distill the mash to obtain 24 to 26 parts of aromatic alcohol, depending on the alcohol strength of the wine.
Dissolve 1 part of powdered sugar in this alcohol.
notes_en: This brandy has the flavor of Kirsch liqueur or may be confused with black cherry brandy.

Slivovitz is a product of Austria-Hungary, distilled from plums (sometimes with added wine or grape refuse) after fermentation. The pits of the plums impart a bitter almond flavor to Slivovitz, and in other respects it resembles cognac to a certain degree. It is considered a Hungarian national beverage, prepared with great care. The price of fine aged varieties approaches that of cognac.
steps_en: Prepare as indicated for Kirschenwasser, using plums instead of cherries, possibly with the addition of wine or grape refuse after fermentation.

yield.note: proportions per liter of fine alcohol

ingredients.note: proportion not clearly specified in text; marked as ambiguous

steps_en: For each liter of fine alcohol (45–50%), add 15–20 g of refined sugar and 25–50 g of one of the essences listed below.
Add a proportion of true fine Slivovitz.
ocr_flags: Slivovitz proportion (text shows '/■>- *\ di Slibowitz' — unclear OCR)

ingredients.note: OCR reads '000'; interpreted as 1000 g or equivalent liquid measure

steps_en: Combine carob, licorice root, table salt, and concentrated raspberry water.
Macerate or infuse in 90° alcohol (1000 g total).
Use as essence in Slivovitz imitation formula.
